---
title: "Week 8: Mar 16 - Mar 22, 2026"
subtitle: "Unit III: Servlets & JSP Introduction"
---

## Week Overview

This week introduces server-side programming with Servlets and Java Server Pages (JSP), covering architecture, lifecycle, and request/response handling.

**Course Outcome:** CO-3 (K3 - Application)  
**Duration:** 1 week (4 lectures + 1 lab)  
**Lab:** Servlet Development  
**Home Assignment 3 Due:** Mar 18

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Quick Practice Quizzes (Lectures 21-24)
Use the [Quiz Hub](/quiz/index.qmd) or jump directly to:

- [Lecture 21 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-21-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 22 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-22-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 23 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-23-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 24 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-24-quiz.qmd)
:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
This week uses upcoming lecture decks. Use the [Lectures Index](/lectures/index.qmd) for all available slides.
:::

---

## Lecture 21: DOM Manipulation using JavaScript {#lecture-21}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-21.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-21.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### DOM Essentials
- DOM as a tree representation of HTML
- Selecting elements with `getElementById` and `querySelector`
- Updating text, attributes, and classes dynamically
- Creating and removing elements using `createElement` and `appendChild`

### Event-Driven Interactivity
```javascript
const btn = document.querySelector('#saveBtn');
btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  document.querySelector('#status').textContent = 'Saved';
});
```

### Practical Use Cases
- Live character counters
- Dynamic lists and cards
- Input-driven UI updates
- Lightweight client-side validation

---

## Lecture 22: Revision & Mini Project (JavaScript & DOM) {#lecture-22}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-22.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-22.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Revision Focus Areas
- Variables, scope, and function reuse
- DOM selection and state updates
- Event handling and form validation
- Class-based UI state (`classList.add/remove/toggle`)

### Mini Project: Smart Task Tracker
Build a single-page app where students can:

- Add task items
- Mark tasks complete
- Show pending task count
- Prevent empty submissions
- Extend with localStorage as bonus

### Sample Core Logic
```javascript
const input = document.querySelector('#taskInput');
const list = document.querySelector('#taskList');

function addTask() {
  const text = input.value.trim();
  if (!text) return;
  const li = document.createElement('li');
  li.textContent = text;
  list.appendChild(li);
  input.value = '';
}
```

---

## Lecture 23: Servlet Overview & Architecture {#lecture-23}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-23.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-23.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Servlet Architecture Components
- Client browser and HTTP request
- Web server and servlet container
- Servlet class for request processing
- Optional database integration layer

### Core Responsibilities
- Parse incoming request data
- Execute business logic
- Generate response (HTML, JSON, redirects)
- Coordinate with service and persistence layers

### Typical Flow
```
Browser -> Web Server -> Servlet Container -> Servlet -> Database
Browser <- Web Server <- Servlet Container <- Servlet <- Database
```

---

## Lecture 24: Servlet Interface & Life Cycle {#lecture-24}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-24-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-24.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-24.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Servlet Interface Methods
- `init(ServletConfig config)`
- `service(ServletRequest req, ServletResponse res)`
- `destroy()`
- `getServletConfig()`
- `getServletInfo()`

### Lifecycle Sequence
```
Load class -> Instantiate -> init() -> service() for each request -> destroy()
```

### Lifecycle Notes
- `init()` runs once during startup
- `service()` runs per request (delegates to doGet/doPost in HttpServlet)
- `destroy()` runs once before removal
- Avoid unsafe mutable shared fields due to multi-threaded requests

---

## Lab 6: Servlet Development

### Objectives
- Create functional servlets
- Handle GET/POST requests
- Manage sessions
- Basic JSP pages

### Activities
1. Create login servlet
2. Implement form processing
3. Add session management
4. Create simple JSP page
5. Deploy to application server

### Deliverables
- Working servlet application
- Functional login system
- Session management

---

## Key Takeaways

✅ Servlets run on server and process requests  
✅ Lifecycle manages servlet creation/destruction  
✅ GET for safe, bookmarkable requests  
✅ POST for secure data submission  
✅ Sessions track user across requests  
✅ JSP simplifies server-side programming

---

## Next Week

Unit III continues:
- JSP implicit objects and directives
- JSTL (JSP Standard Tag Library)
- MVC architecture
- Database connectivity
